I'll start as the dental receptionist; you reply as the patient. — What evidence standard supports this intervention now versus watchful waiting, what conflicts of interest exist in-house, and how do you document if I defer?

scenarios.title: Pet sick
scenarios.setting: Your pet is not well. You see the vet.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Pet sick. Your pet is not well. You see the vet. I'll start as the veterinarian; you reply as the pet owner. — Hello. My dog is sick. She will not eat. Can you help?

scenarios.title: Vet check-up
scenarios.setting: You describe symptoms and diet changes.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Vet check-up. You describe symptoms and diet changes. I'll start as the veterinarian; you reply as the pet owner. — He has been vomiting since yesterday — still drinking water — should I bring him in today?

scenarios.title: Treatment options and cost
scenarios.setting: You weigh tests versus watchful waiting.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Treatment options and cost. You weigh tests versus watchful waiting. I'll start as the veterinarian; you reply as the pet owner. — What differential you consider likely, what diagnostics change the plan, and what ballpark cost before we proceed?

scenarios.title: Chronic condition maintenance
scenarios.setting: Meds stopped working as well.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Chronic condition maintenance. Meds stopped working as well. I'll start as the veterinarian; you reply as the pet owner. — What trend lines concern you in her labs, what titration strategy you recommend, and what side effects mean we stop?

scenarios.title: End-of-life care framing
scenarios.setting: Quality of life is ambiguous; you seek clarity.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: End-of-life care framing. Quality of life is ambiguous; you seek clarity. I'll start as the veterinarian; you reply as the pet owner. — What objective pain signals you watch, what timeline honesty you can give, and what hospice-style supports exist at home?

scenarios.title: Ethical tension in expensive care
scenarios.setting: Finances and love collide; you navigate without shame.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Ethical tension in expensive care. Finances and love collide; you navigate without shame. I'll start as the veterinarian; you reply as the pet owner. — What minimum care preserves dignity, what optional heroic measures have low success, and how do we decide without guilt-tripping family members?

scenarios.title: Phone plan
scenarios.setting: You buy a phone plan. You need simple help.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Phone plan. You buy a phone plan. You need simple help. I'll start as the mobile shop staff; you reply as the customer. — Hello. I need a phone plan. I use internet every day. What is good?

scenarios.title: SIM and data
scenarios.setting: You compare two prepaid options.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: SIM and data. You compare two prepaid options. I'll start as the mobile shop staff; you reply as the customer. — I need about 20 GB and some international minutes — what plan fits without a contract?

scenarios.title: Family plan trade-offs
scenarios.setting: Multiple lines; you clarify throttling rules.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Family plan trade-offs. Multiple lines; you clarify throttling rules. I'll start as the mobile shop staff; you reply as the customer. — What happens after the cap, whether hotspot is included, and what roaming charges look like in region B?

scenarios.title: Contract fine print
scenarios.setting: Promotional price expires; you probe renewal.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Contract fine print. Promotional price expires; you probe renewal. I'll start as the mobile shop staff; you reply as the customer. — What price after month twelve, what exit fees, and what network priority differences matter for my work VPN?

scenarios.title: Privacy versus carrier bundles
scenarios.setting: You question data resale and DNS.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Privacy versus carrier bundles. You question data resale and DNS. I'll start as the mobile shop staff; you reply as the customer. — What data you monetize, what opt-outs exist without degrading service, and what technical setup reduces tracking on-device?

scenarios.title: Regulatory roaming dispute
scenarios.setting: Bill shock after travel; you argue consumer protection.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Regulatory roaming dispute. Bill shock after travel; you argue consumer protection. I'll start as the mobile shop staff; you reply as the customer. — What disclosure you claim versus what UX showed, what good-faith adjustment policy exists, and what ombudsman path you expect if we disagree?

scenarios.title: Rental key
scenarios.setting: You arrive at a short rental.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Rental key. You arrive at a short rental. I'll start as the host; you reply as the guest. — Hello. I am here. Where is the key? The code does not work.

scenarios.title: Check-in problem
scenarios.setting: Lockbox code fails; you message the host.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Check-in problem. Lockbox code fails; you message the host. I'll start as the host; you reply as the guest. — We are at the door — the code you sent does not open the box; can you confirm or send someone?

scenarios.title: Listing mismatch
scenarios.setting: Photos showed amenities missing.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Listing mismatch. Photos showed amenities missing. I'll start as the host; you reply as the guest. — The Wi-Fi is unusable for work — what fix timeline you commit to, and what partial refund policy applies if it spans the stay?

scenarios.title: Neighborhood safety surprise
scenarios.setting: Noise and access differ from description.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Neighborhood safety surprise. Noise and access differ from description. I'll start as the host; you reply as the guest. — What you knew about night noise, what disclosure you consider adequate, and how we document this for platform support?

scenarios.title: Host-guest power imbalance
scenarios.setting: Threat of bad review flies; you de-escalate.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Host-guest power imbalance. Threat of bad review flies; you de-escalate. I'll start as the host; you reply as the guest. — What remedy restores fairness without retaliation risk, what platform rules govern review coercion, and what neutral evidence we should collect now?

scenarios.title: Short-term rental regulation edge
scenarios.setting: Building policy may prohibit; you clarify liability.
scenarios.firstLine: Scene: Short-term rental regulation edge. Building policy may prohibit; you clarify liability. I'll start as the host; you reply as the guest. — What legal risk you carry versus me as guest, what insurance gaps exist, and what exit with minimum harm if authorities intervene?
